description: OWASP WSTG v4.1 checklist + network/cloud test items

items.canonical_name: Conduct Search Engine Discovery Reconnaissance for Information Leakage
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Conduct Search Engine Discovery Reconnaissance for Information Leakage
Use a search engine to search for Network diagrams and Configurations, Credentials, Error message content.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Use a search engine to search for Network diagrams and Configurations, Credentials, Error message content.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Fingerprint Web Server
Find the version and type of a running web server to determine known vulnerabilities and the appropriate exploits. Using "HTTP header field ordering" and "Malformed requests test".
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Find the version and type of a running web server to determine known vulnerabilities and the appropriate exploits. Using "HTTP header field ordering" and "Malformed requests test".

items.canonical_name: Review Webserver Metafiles for Information Leakage
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Review Webserver Metafiles for Information Leakage
Analyze robots.txt and identify <META> Tags from website.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Analyze robots.txt and identify <META> Tags from website.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Enumerate Applications on Webserver
Find applications hosted in the webserver (Virtual hosts/Subdomain), non-standard ports, DNS zone transfers
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Find applications hosted in the webserver (Virtual hosts/Subdomain), non-standard ports, DNS zone transfers

items.canonical_name: Review Webpage Comments and Metadata for Information Leakage
aliases: review webpage comments and metadata for
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Review Webpage Comments and Metadata for Information Leakage
Find sensitive information from webpage comments and Metadata on source code.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Find sensitive information from webpage comments and Metadata on source code.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Identify application entry points
Identify from hidden fields, parameters, methods HTTP header analysis
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Identify from hidden fields, parameters, methods HTTP header analysis

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Map execution paths through application
Map the target application and understand the principal workflows.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Map the target application and understand the principal workflows.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Fingerprint Web Application Framework
Find the type of web application framework/CMS from HTTP headers, Cookies, Source code, Specific files and folders.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Find the type of web application framework/CMS from HTTP headers, Cookies, Source code, Specific files and folders.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Fingerprint Web Application
Identify the web application and version to determine known vulnerabilities and the appropriate exploits.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Identify the web application and version to determine known vulnerabilities and the appropriate exploits.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Map Application Architecture
Identify application architecture including Web language, WAF, Reverse proxy, Application Server, Backend Database
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Identify application architecture including Web language, WAF, Reverse proxy, Application Server, Backend Database

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Network/Infrastructure Configuration
Understand the infrastructure elements interactions, config management for software, backend DB server, WebDAV, FTP in order to identify known vulnerabilities.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Understand the infrastructure elements interactions, config management for software, backend DB server, WebDAV, FTP in order to identify known vulnerabilities.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Application Platform Configuration
Identify default installation file/directory, Handle Server errors (40*,50*), Minimal Privilege, Software logging.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Identify default installation file/directory, Handle Server errors (40*,50*), Minimal Privilege, Software logging.

items.canonical_name: Test File Extensions Handling for Sensitive Information
aliases: test file extensions handling for sensit
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test File Extensions Handling for Sensitive Information
Find important file, information (.asa , .inc , .sql ,zip, tar, pdf, txt, etc)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Find important file, information (.asa , .inc , .sql ,zip, tar, pdf, txt, etc)

items.canonical_name: Backup and Unreferenced Files for Sensitive Information
aliases: backup and unreferenced files for sensit
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Backup and Unreferenced Files for Sensitive Information
Check JS source code, comments, cache file, backup file (.old, .bak, .inc, .src) and guessing of filename
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check JS source code, comments, cache file, backup file (.old, .bak, .inc, .src) and guessing of filename

items.canonical_name: Enumerate Infrastructure and Application Admin Interfaces
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Enumerate Infrastructure and Application Admin Interfaces
Directory and file enumeration, comments and links in source (/admin, /administrator, /backoffice, /backend, etc), alternative server port (Tomcat/8080)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Directory and file enumeration, comments and links in source (/admin, /administrator, /backoffice, /backend, etc), alternative server port (Tomcat/8080)

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test HTTP Methods
Identify HTTP allowed methods on Web server with OPTIONS. Arbitrary HTTP Methods, HEAD access control bypass and XST
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Identify HTTP allowed methods on Web server with OPTIONS. Arbitrary HTTP Methods, HEAD access control bypass and XST

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test HTTP Strict Transport Security
Identify HSTS header on Web server through HTTP response header.  curl -s -D- https://domain.com/ | grep Strict
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Identify HSTS header on Web server through HTTP response header.  curl -s -D- https://domain.com/ | grep Strict

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test RIA cross domain policy
Analyse the permissions allowed from the policy files (crossdomain.xml/clientaccesspolicy.xml) and allow-access-from.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Analyse the permissions allowed from the policy files (crossdomain.xml/clientaccesspolicy.xml) and allow-access-from.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test File Permission
In Linux, use ls command to check the file permissions. Alternatively, namei can also be used to recursively list file permissions
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: In Linux, use ls command to check the file permissions. Alternatively, namei can also be used to recursively list file permissions

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test for Subdomain Takeover

The first step is to enumerate the victim DNS servers and resource records. There are multiple ways to accomplish this task, for example DNS enumeration using a list of common subdomains dictionary, DNS brute force or using web search engines and other OSINT data sources
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: The first step is to enumerate the victim DNS servers and resource records. There are multiple ways to accomplish this task, for example DNS enumeration using a list of common subdomains dictionary, DNS brute force or using web search engines and other OSINT data sources

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Cloud Storage
Assess whether Cloud Storage Service's access control configuration is properly in place.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Assess whether Cloud Storage Service's access control configuration is properly in place.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Role Definitions
Validate the system roles defined within the application by creating permission matrix.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Validate the system roles defined within the application by creating permission matrix.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test User Registration Process
Verify that the identity requirements for user registration are aligned with business and security requirements:
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Verify that the identity requirements for user registration are aligned with business and security requirements:

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Account Provisioning Process
Determine which roles are able to provision users and what sort of accounts they can provision.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Determine which roles are able to provision users and what sort of accounts they can provision.

items.canonical_name: Testing for Account Enumeration and Guessable User Account
aliases: testing for account enumeration and gues
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Account Enumeration and Guessable User Account
Generic login error statement check, return codes/parameter values, enumerate all possible valid userids (Login system, Forgot password)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Generic login error statement check, return codes/parameter values, enumerate all possible valid userids (Login system, Forgot password)

items.canonical_name: Testing for Weak or unenforced username policy
aliases: testing for weak or unenforced username
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Weak or unenforced username policy
User account names are often highly structured (e.g. Joe Bloggs account name is jbloggs and Fred Nurks account name is fnurks) and valid account names can easily be guessed.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: User account names are often highly structured (e.g. Joe Bloggs account name is jbloggs and Fred Nurks account name is fnurks) and valid account names can easily be guessed.

items.canonical_name: Testing for Credentials Transported over an Encrypted Channel
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Credentials Transported over an Encrypted Channel
Check referrer whether its HTTP or HTTPs. Sending data through HTTP and HTTPS.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check referrer whether its HTTP or HTTPs. Sending data through HTTP and HTTPS.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for default credentials
Testing for default credentials of common applications, Testing for default password of new accounts.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Testing for default credentials of common applications, Testing for default password of new accounts.

items.canonical_name: Testing for Weak lock out mechanism
aliases: testing for weak lock out mechanism
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Weak lock out mechanism
Evaluate the account lockout mechanism’s ability to mitigate brute force password guessing. Evaluate the unlock mechanism’s resistance to unauthorized account unlocking.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Evaluate the account lockout mechanism’s ability to mitigate brute force password guessing. Evaluate the unlock mechanism’s resistance to unauthorized account unlocking.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for bypassing authentication schema
Force browsing (/admin/main.php, /page.asp?authenticated=yes), Parameter Modification, Session ID prediction, SQL Injection
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Force browsing (/admin/main.php, /page.asp?authenticated=yes), Parameter Modification, Session ID prediction, SQL Injection

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test remember password functionality
Look for passwords being stored in a cookie. Examine the cookies stored by the application. Verify that the credentials are not stored in clear text, but are hashed. Autocompleted=off?
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Look for passwords being stored in a cookie. Examine the cookies stored by the application. Verify that the credentials are not stored in clear text, but are hashed. Autocompleted=off?

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Browser cache weakness
Check browser history issue by clicking "Back" button after logging out. Check browser cache issue from HTTP response headers (Cache-Control: no-cache)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check browser history issue by clicking "Back" button after logging out. Check browser cache issue from HTTP response headers (Cache-Control: no-cache)

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Weak password policy

Determine the resistance of the application against brute force password guessing using available password dictionaries by evaluating the length, complexity, reuse and aging requirements of passwords.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Determine the resistance of the application against brute force password guessing using available password dictionaries by evaluating the length, complexity, reuse and aging requirements of passwords.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Weak security question/answer
Testing for weak pre-generated questions, Testing for weak self-generated question, Testing for brute-forcible answers (Unlimited attempts?)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Testing for weak pre-generated questions, Testing for weak self-generated question, Testing for brute-forcible answers (Unlimited attempts?)

items.canonical_name: Testing for weak password change or reset functionalities
aliases: testing for weak password change or rese
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for weak password change or reset functionalities
Test password reset (Display old password in plain-text?, Send via email?, Random token on confirmation email ?), Test password change (Need old password?), CSRF vulnerability ?
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Test password reset (Display old password in plain-text?, Send via email?, Random token on confirmation email ?), Test password change (Need old password?), CSRF vulnerability ?

items.canonical_name: Testing for Weaker authentication in alternative channel
aliases: testing for weaker authentication in alt
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Weaker authentication in alternative channel
Understand the primary mechanism and Identify other channels (Mobile App, Call center, SSO)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Understand the primary mechanism and Identify other channels (Mobile App, Call center, SSO)

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing Directory Traversal - File Include
dot-dot-slash attack (../), Directory traversal, Local File inclusion/Remote File Inclusion.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: dot-dot-slash attack (../), Directory traversal, Local File inclusion/Remote File Inclusion.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for bypassing authorization schema
Access a resource without authentication?, Bypass ACL, Force browsing (/admin/adduser.jsp)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Access a resource without authentication?, Bypass ACL, Force browsing (/admin/adduser.jsp)

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Privilege Escalation
Testing for role/privilege manipulate the values of hidden variables. Change some param groupid=2 to groupid=1
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Testing for role/privilege manipulate the values of hidden variables. Change some param groupid=2 to groupid=1

items.canonical_name: Testing for Insecure Direct Object References
aliases: testing for insecure direct object refer
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Insecure Direct Object References
Force changing parameter value (?invoice=123 -> ?invoice=456)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Force changing parameter value (?invoice=123 -> ?invoice=456)

items.canonical_name: Testing for Bypassing Session Management Schema
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Bypassing Session Management Schema
SessionID analysis prediction, unencrypted cookie transport, brute-force.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: SessionID analysis prediction, unencrypted cookie transport, brute-force.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Cookies attributes
Check HTTPOnly and Secure flag, expiration, inspect for sensitive data.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check HTTPOnly and Secure flag, expiration, inspect for sensitive data.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Session Fixation
The application doesn't renew the cookie after a successfully user authentication.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: The application doesn't renew the cookie after a successfully user authentication.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Exposed Session Variables
Encryption & Reuse of session Tokens vulnerabilities, Send sessionID with GET method ?
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Encryption & Reuse of session Tokens vulnerabilities, Send sessionID with GET method ?

items.canonical_name: Testing for Cross Site Request Forgery
aliases: testing for cross site request forgery
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Cross Site Request Forgery
URL analysis, Direct access to functions without any token.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: URL analysis, Direct access to functions without any token.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for logout functionality
Check reuse session after logout both server-side and SSO.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check reuse session after logout both server-side and SSO.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Session Timeout
Check session timeout, after the timeout has passed, all session tokens should be destroyed or be unusable.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check session timeout, after the timeout has passed, all session tokens should be destroyed or be unusable.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Session puzzling

The application uses the same session variable for more than one purpose. An attacker can potentially access pages in an order unanticipated by the developers so that the session variable is set in one context and then used in another.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: The application uses the same session variable for more than one purpose. An attacker can potentially access pages in an order unanticipated by the developers so that the session variable is set in one context and then used in another.

items.canonical_name: Testing for Reflected Cross Site Scripting
aliases: testing for reflected cross site scripti
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Reflected Cross Site Scripting
Check for input validation, Replace the vector used to identify XSS, XSS with HTTP Parameter Pollution.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check for input validation, Replace the vector used to identify XSS, XSS with HTTP Parameter Pollution.

items.canonical_name: Testing for Stored Cross Site Scripting
aliases: testing for stored cross site scripting
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Stored Cross Site Scripting
Check input forms/Upload forms and analyze HTML codes, Leverage XSS with BeEF
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check input forms/Upload forms and analyze HTML codes, Leverage XSS with BeEF

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for HTTP Verb Tampering
Craft custom HTTP requests to test the other methods to bypass URL authentication and authorization.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Craft custom HTTP requests to test the other methods to bypass URL authentication and authorization.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for HTTP Parameter pollution
Identify any form or action that allows user-supplied input to bypass Input validation and filters using HPP
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Identify any form or action that allows user-supplied input to bypass Input validation and filters using HPP

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for SQL Injection
Union, Boolean, Error based, Out-of-band, Time delay.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Union, Boolean, Error based, Out-of-band, Time delay.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for LDAP Injection
/ldapsearch?user=* user=*user=*)(uid=*))(|(uid=* pass=password
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: /ldapsearch?user=* user=*user=*)(uid=*))(|(uid=* pass=password

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for XML Injection
Check with XML Meta Characters ', " , <>, <!--/-->, &, <![CDATA[ / ]]>, XXE, TAG
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check with XML Meta Characters ', " , <>, <!--/-->, &, <![CDATA[ / ]]>, XXE, TAG

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for SSI Injection
• Presense of .shtml extension • Check for these characters < ! # = / . " - > and [a-zA-Z0-9] • include String = <!--#include virtual="/etc/passwd" -->
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Presense of .shtml extension • Check for these characters < ! # = / . " - > and [a-zA-Z0-9] • include String = <!--#include virtual="/etc/passwd" -->

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for XPath Injection
Check for XML error enumeration by supplying a single quote (') Username: ‘ or ‘1’ = ‘1 Password: ‘ or ‘1’ = ‘1
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check for XML error enumeration by supplying a single quote (') Username: ‘ or ‘1’ = ‘1 Password: ‘ or ‘1’ = ‘1

test_steps: Identify test scope for: IMAP/SMTP Injection

• Identifying vulnerable parameters with special characters (i.e.: \, ‘, “, @, #, !, |) • Understanding the data flow and deployment structure of the client • IMAP/SMTP command injection (Header, Body, Footer)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Identifying vulnerable parameters with special characters (i.e.: \, ‘, “, @, #, !, |) • Understanding the data flow and deployment structure of the client • IMAP/SMTP command injection (Header, Body, Footer)

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Code Injection
Enter OS commands in the input field. ?arg=1; system('id')
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Enter OS commands in the input field. ?arg=1; system('id')

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Command Injection
Understand the application platform, OS, folder structure, relative path and execute OS commands on a Web server. %3Bcat%20/etc/passwd test.pdf+|+Dir C:\
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Understand the application platform, OS, folder structure, relative path and execute OS commands on a Web server. %3Bcat%20/etc/passwd test.pdf+|+Dir C:\

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Buffer overflow
• Testing for heap overflow vulnerability • Testing for stack overflow vulnerability • Testing for format string vulnerability
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Testing for heap overflow vulnerability • Testing for stack overflow vulnerability • Testing for format string vulnerability

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for incubated vulnerabilities
File Upload, Stored XSS , SQL/XPATH Injection, Misconfigured servers (Tomcat, Plesk, Cpanel)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: File Upload, Stored XSS , SQL/XPATH Injection, Misconfigured servers (Tomcat, Plesk, Cpanel)

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for HTTP Splitting/Smuggling
param=foobar%0d%0aContent-Length:%200%0d%0a%0d%0aHTTP/1.1%20200%20OK%0d%0aContent-Type:%20text/html%0d%0aContent-Length:%2035%0d%0a%0d%0a<html>Sorry,%20System%20Down</html>
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: param=foobar%0d%0aContent-Length:%200%0d%0a%0d%0aHTTP/1.1%20200%20OK%0d%0aContent-Type:%20text/html%0d%0aContent-Length:%2035%0d%0a%0d%0a<html>Sorry,%20System%20Down</html>

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for HTTP Incoming Requests
Monitor all incoming and outgoing HTTP requests to the Web Server to inspect any suspicious requests. Monitor HTTP traffic without changes of end user Browser proxy or client-side application.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Monitor all incoming and outgoing HTTP requests to the Web Server to inspect any suspicious requests. Monitor HTTP traffic without changes of end user Browser proxy or client-side application.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Host Header Injection

initial testing is as simple as supplying another domain (i.e. attacker.com) into the Host header field. It is how the web server processes the header value that dictates the impact. The attack is valid when the web server processes the input to send the request to an attacker-controlled host that resides at the supplied domain, and not to an internal virtual host that resides on the web server.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: initial testing is as simple as supplying another domain (i.e. attacker.com) into the Host header field. It is how the web server processes the header value that dictates the impact. The attack is valid when the web server processes the input to send the request to an attacker-controlled host that resides at the supplied domain, and not to an internal virtual host that resides on the web server.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Server-side Template Injection

SSTI vulnerabilities exist either in text or code context. In plaintext context users allowed to use freeform 'text' with direct HTML code. In code context the user input may also be placed within a template statement (eg. in a variable name). In both cases the testing methodology has the following steps:
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: SSTI vulnerabilities exist either in text or code context. In plaintext context users allowed to use freeform 'text' with direct HTML code. In code context the user input may also be placed within a template statement (eg. in a variable name). In both cases the testing methodology has the following steps:

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Analysis of Error Codes
Locate error codes generated from applications or web servers. Collect sensitive information from that errors (Web Server, Application Server, Database)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Locate error codes generated from applications or web servers. Collect sensitive information from that errors (Web Server, Application Server, Database)

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Analysis of Stack Traces
• Invalid Input / Empty inputs • Input that contains non alphanumeric characters or query syn tax • Access to internal pages without authentication • Bypassing application flow
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Invalid Input / Empty inputs • Input that contains non alphanumeric characters or query syn tax • Access to internal pages without authentication • Bypassing application flow

items.canonical_name: Testing for Weak SSL/TSL Ciphers, Insufficient Transport Layer Protection
aliases: testing for weak ssl/tsl ciphers, insuff
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Weak SSL/TSL Ciphers, Insufficient Transport Layer Protection
Identify SSL service, Idectify weak ciphers/protocols (ie. RC4, BEAST, CRIME, POODLE)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Identify SSL service, Idectify weak ciphers/protocols (ie. RC4, BEAST, CRIME, POODLE)

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Padding Oracle

Compare the responses in three different states: • Cipher text gets decrypted, resulting data is correct. • Cipher text gets decrypted, resulting data is garbled and causes some exception or error handling in the application logic. • Cipher text decryption fails due to padding errors.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Compare the responses in three different states: • Cipher text gets decrypted, resulting data is correct. • Cipher text gets decrypted, resulting data is garbled and causes some exception or error handling in the application logic. • Cipher text decryption fails due to padding errors.

items.canonical_name: Testing for Sensitive information sent via unencrypted channels
aliases: testing for sensitive information sent v
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Sensitive information sent via unencrypted channels

Check sensitive data during the transmission: • Information used in authentication (e.g. Credentials, PINs, Session identifiers, Tokens, Cookies…) • Information protected by laws, regulations or specific organizational policy (e.g. Credit Cards, Customers data)
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Check sensitive data during the transmission: • Information used in authentication (e.g. Credentials, PINs, Session identifiers, Tokens, Cookies…) • Information protected by laws, regulations or specific organizational policy (e.g. Credit Cards, Customers data)

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Weak Encryption

Incorrect uses of encryption algorithm may result in sensitive data exposure, key leakage, broken authentication, insecure session and spoofing attack. There are some encryption or hash algorithm is known to be weak and not suggested to be used anymore such as MD5 and RC4.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Incorrect uses of encryption algorithm may result in sensitive data exposure, key leakage, broken authentication, insecure session and spoofing attack. There are some encryption or hash algorithm is known to be weak and not suggested to be used anymore such as MD5 and RC4.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Business Logic Data Validation
• Looking for data entry points or hand off points between systems or software. • Once found try to insert logically invalid data into the application/system.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Looking for data entry points or hand off points between systems or software. • Once found try to insert logically invalid data into the application/system.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Ability to Forge Requests

• Looking for guessable, predictable or hidden functionality of fields. • Once found try to insert logically valid data into the application/system allowing the user go through the application/system against the normal busineess logic workflow.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Looking for guessable, predictable or hidden functionality of fields. • Once found try to insert logically valid data into the application/system allowing the user go through the application/system against the normal busineess logic workflow.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Integrity Checks

•Looking for parts of the application/system (components i.e. For example, input fields, databases or logs) that move, store or handle data/information. • For each identified component determine what type of data/information is logically acceptable and what types the application/system should guard against. Also, consider who according to the business logic is allowed to insert, update and delete data/information and in each component. • Attempt to insert, update or edit delete the data/information values with invalid data/information into each component (i.e. input, database, or log) by users that .should not be allowed per the busines logic workflow.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: •Looking for parts of the application/system (components i.e. For example, input fields, databases or logs) that move, store or handle data/information. • For each identified component determine what type of data/information is logically acceptable and what types the application/system should guard against. Also, consider who according to the business logic is allowed to insert, update and delete data/information and in each component. • Attempt to insert, update or edit delete the data/information values with invalid data/information into each component (i.e. input, database, or log) by users that .should not be allowed per the busines logic workflow.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test for Process Timing

• Looking for application/system functionality that may be impacted by time. Such as execution time or actions that help users predict a future outcome or allow one to circumvent any part of the business logic or workflow. For example, not completing transactions in an expected time. • Develop and execute the mis-use cases ensuring that attackers can not gain an advantage based on any timing.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Looking for application/system functionality that may be impacted by time. Such as execution time or actions that help users predict a future outcome or allow one to circumvent any part of the business logic or workflow. For example, not completing transactions in an expected time. • Develop and execute the mis-use cases ensuring that attackers can not gain an advantage based on any timing.

items.canonical_name: Test Number of Times a Function Can be Used Limits
aliases: test number of times a function can be u
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Number of Times a Function Can be Used Limits

• Looking for functions or features in the application or system that should not be executed more that a single time or specified number of times during the business logic workflow. • For each of the functions and features found that should only be executed a single time or specified number of times during the business logic workflow, develop abuse/misuse cases that may allow a user to execute more than the allowable number of times.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Looking for functions or features in the application or system that should not be executed more that a single time or specified number of times during the business logic workflow. • For each of the functions and features found that should only be executed a single time or specified number of times during the business logic workflow, develop abuse/misuse cases that may allow a user to execute more than the allowable number of times.

items.canonical_name: Testing for the Circumvention of Work Flows
aliases: testing for the circumvention of work fl
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for the Circumvention of Work Flows

• Looking for methods to skip or go to steps in the application process in a different order from the designed/intended business logic flow. • For each method develop a misuse case and try to circumvent or perform an action that is "not acceptable" per the the business logic workflow.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Looking for methods to skip or go to steps in the application process in a different order from the designed/intended business logic flow. • For each method develop a misuse case and try to circumvent or perform an action that is "not acceptable" per the the business logic workflow.

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Defenses Against Application Mis-use
Measures that might indicate the application has in-built self-defense: • Changed responses • Blocked requests • Actions that log a user out or lock their account
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Measures that might indicate the application has in-built self-defense: • Changed responses • Blocked requests • Actions that log a user out or lock their account

items.canonical_name: Test Upload of Unexpected File Types
aliases: test upload of unexpected file types
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Upload of Unexpected File Types

• Review the project documentation and perform some exploratory testing looking for file types that should be "unsupported" by the application/system. • Try to upload these “unsupported” files an verify that it are properly rejected. • If multiple files can be uploaded at once, there must be tests in place to verify that each file is properly evaluated.  PS. file.phtml, shell.phPWND, SHELL~1.PHP
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Review the project documentation and perform some exploratory testing looking for file types that should be "unsupported" by the application/system. • Try to upload these “unsupported” files an verify that it are properly rejected. • If multiple files can be uploaded at once, there must be tests in place to verify that each file is properly evaluated.  PS. file.phtml, shell.phPWND, SHELL~1.PHP

test_steps: Identify test scope for: Test Upload of Malicious Files

• Develop or acquire a known “malicious” file. • Try to upload the malicious file to the application/system and verify that it is correctly rejected. • If multiple files can be uploaded at once, there must be tests in place to verify that each file is properly evaluated.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: • Develop or acquire a known “malicious” file. • Try to upload the malicious file to the application/system and verify that it is correctly rejected. • If multiple files can be uploaded at once, there must be tests in place to verify that each file is properly evaluated.

items.canonical_name: Testing for Cross Site Script Inclusion
aliases: testing for cross site script inclusion
test_steps: Identify test scope for: Testing for Cross Site Script Inclusion

Identify which endpoints are responsible for sending sensitive data, what parameters are required, and identify all relevant dynamically and statically generated JavaScript responses using authenticated user sessions. Pay special attention to sensitive data sent using JSONP. To find dynamically generated JavaScript responses, generate authenticated and unauthenticated requests, then compare them. If they're different, it means the response is dynamic; otherwise it's static. To simplify this task, a tool such as Veit Hailperin's Burp proxy plugin can be used. Make sure to check other file types in addition to JavaScript; XSSI is not limited to JavaScript files alone.
Document findings with request/response evidence
Verify exploitability and assess business impact
items.description: Identify which endpoints are responsible for sending sensitive data, what parameters are required, and identify all relevant dynamically and statically generated JavaScript responses using authenticated user sessions. Pay special attention to sensitive data sent using JSONP. To find dynamically generated JavaScript responses, generate authenticated and unauthenticated requests, then compare them. If they're different, it means the response is dynamic; otherwise it's static. To simplify this task, a tool such as Veit Hailperin's Burp proxy plugin can be used. Make sure to check other file types in addition to JavaScript; XSSI is not limited to JavaScript files alone.

test_steps: Run nmap -sV -sC on target
Identify services on high-risk ports: 21, 22, 23, 25, 445, 3389
Attempt banner grab to confirm service version
Cross-reference version against CVE database
Identify service and version
Attempt known default credentials for that service
For Redis/MongoDB/Elasticsearch: attempt unauthenticated connection
For MySQL: attempt root with blank password
Document successful access

items.canonical_name: Outdated Software Version with Known CVE
test_steps: Identify software version via nmap -sV
Cross-reference against NVD/CVE database
Confirm CVE applicability to exact version
Document CVSS score and exploit availability
Run: nmap --script ssl-enum-ciphers -p 443 target
Check for SSLv3, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1 support
Check for weak cipher suites (RC4, DES, EXPORT)
Check certificate validity and chain
List S3 buckets
Check bucket ACL and policy for public access
Verify Block Public Access settings
Attempt anonymous access to bucket contents
Check if root account has active access keys
Check if MFA is enabled for all console users
Check for overly permissive IAM policies (*:*)
Check password policy strength
Check CloudTrail is enabled in all regions
Verify log file validation is enabled
Check CloudWatch alarms exist for critical API calls
Verify S3 bucket for logs is not publicly accessible

categories: Service version detection and CVE correlation
